title: Order Relations in Cardinal Numbers
description: A visual representation of the fundamental properties that define an order relation on cardinal numbers, showing how they compare to each other.
relationships: a ≤ b is defined by existence of injection f: A→B
Reflexivity: a ≤ a (shown by identity injection A→A)
Antisymmetry: if a ≤ b and b ≤ a then a = b (Cantor-Schröder-Bernstein Theorem)
Transitivity: if a ≤ b and b ≤ c then a ≤ c (composition of injections)
